channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Learners in Eastern Cape Farming Communities Struggle Without Scholar Transport
item.description: In the rural farming communities of Enon and Bersheba, Eastern Cape, over 80 learners have been left without scholar transport this year, forcing them to hitchhike daily to Moses Mabida Secondary Scho...

item.title: Fire at Glasgow Central Station causes building collapse and train service disruption
item.description: On March 8, 2026, a fire broke out in a commercial building near Glasgow Central Station, reportedly starting in a vape shop. The blaze led to the partial collapse of the four-storey structure, raisin...

item.title: Fire at Glasgow Central Station causes building collapse and disrupts train services
item.description: A significant fire broke out at Glasgow Central Station on Sunday afternoon, originating from a vape shop on Union Street. The blaze quickly escalated, causing a partially B-listed Victorian building ...

item.title: Mercedes secure 1-2 finish at Australian Grand Prix with Russell and Antonelli
item.description: Mercedes made a strong statement in the 2026 Formula One season opener at Albert Park, Melbourne, with George Russell taking the win ahead of teammate Kimi Antonelli. The Briton’s first victory at the...

item.title: BB Bread knock out Gor Mahia after earlier upset against AFC Leopards in FKF Cup
item.description: This article covers how little‑known side BB Bread FC continued their remarkable run in the FKF Cup by eliminating Kenyan giants Gor Mahia, only weeks after shocking AFC Leopards in the same competiti...

item.title: Preparations and Safety Measures Ahead of the Safari Rally in Naivasha
item.description: The World Rally Championship (WRC) Safari Rally is set to take place in Naivasha over four days starting Thursday, featuring 46 drivers. This year marks the first time since 1953 that the ceremonial s...

item.title: Two drivers die in four-vehicle collision on A370 near Bristol
item.description: Two individuals have tragically lost their lives following a four-vehicle collision on the A370 near Yanley, close to Long Ashton, Bristol. The incident occurred at approximately 7:45pm on Saturday ev...

item.title: Family of mother and three children illegally detained in Antipolo home due to land dispute
item.description: In Barangay Cupang, Antipolo City, Rizal, a mother and her three children were held captive for several hours inside their own house last Saturday. Four men, armed with bolos, padlocked the gate of th...

item.title: Concerns Raised Over Pete Hegseth’s Conduct as US Defence Secretary Amid Iran Conflict
item.description: Pete Hegseth, former Fox News host, has sparked alarm in his first week as US Secretary of Defence under President Donald Trump, following the escalation of conflict with Iran. Critics describe Hegset...

item.title: National Treasury revises SABC's 2026/27 outlook to R41.3m loss instead of projected profit
item.description: The South African Broadcasting Corporation (SABC) has effectively dropped its ambitious target of achieving a R1 billion profit in the 2026/27 financial year. According to the National Treasury's late...

item.title: Homemade explosive device thrown near anti-Islam protest outside NYC mayor’s residence
item.description: On Saturday, two improvised explosive devices (IEDs) were thrown near a protest outside Gracie Mansion, the official residence of New York City Mayor Zohran Mamdani. The protest, organised by far-righ...

item.title: Yanga Dominates Polisi 7–1 to Secure Spot in CRDB Federation Cup Round of 16
item.description: On Sunday night at KMC Complex, Young Africans (Yanga) showed serious class as they overwhelmed Polisi Tanzania FC 7–1 in the CRDB Federation Cup. Right from the first minute, midfielder Mudathir Yahy...

item.title: Clashes erupt between Rangers and Celtic supporters following Scottish Cup match
item.description: Following the Scottish Cup quarter-final between Rangers and Celtic at Ibrox Stadium, violent clashes broke out on the pitch involving supporters from both teams. The match ended in a goalless draw af...

item.title: Vihiga Ambulance Driver Henry Tira Fatally Attacked While Heading Home
item.description: Vihiga County is in deep mourning following the tragic death of Henry Tira, a dedicated ambulance driver at Vihiga Teaching and Referral Hospital. Known for his humble and friendly nature, Henry spent...

item.title: Iran appoints new supreme leader amid escalating US-Israel conflict
item.description: Iran has officially named Mojtaba Khamenei, son of the late Ayatollah Ali Khamenei, as its new supreme leader. The announcement comes as Iran faces extensive attacks from both the US and Israel, targe...

item.title: Mollie Jepsen Withdraws from Remaining Milano-Cortina Paralympic Events After Knee Injury
item.description: Mollie Jepsen, a prominent Canadian Para alpine skier from West Vancouver, B.C., has ended her participation in the Milano-Cortina Paralympic Games early due to a knee injury. The Canadian Paralympic ...

item.title: Chris Briney’s life and relationship with actress Isabel Machado
item.description: Chris Briney, born Christopher Thomas Briney on 24 March 1998 in Danbury, Connecticut, is an American actor known for roles in 'The Summer I Turned Pretty' and 'Mean Girls'. His dad, Michael Briney, a...

item.title: PCSO and MMDA deliver relief aid to Quezon City fire victims
item.description: Some 1,907 families affected by a fire in Barangay Pinyahan, Quezon City, have received immediate relief assistance from the Philippine Charity Sweepstakes Office (PCSO) in partnership with the Metrop...

item.title: Couple successfully registers small strip of garden land after legal dispute
item.description: A couple in Westcott, Surrey, have successfully won a legal dispute over a small patch of grass outside their home, after their neighbours attempted to claim it with the placement of a garden gnome. E...

item.title: Wings of Compassion Foundation Gives School Bags and Book Dividers to Pupils in Oyo
item.description: The Wings of Compassion Foundation (WINCOF) don carry out charitable act for pupils wey no too get in Oyo town. Dem distribute 250 strong school bags plus plenty book dividers to children for two prim...
